top_signals.title: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop as Trump, Iran issue mixed messages on talks in Qatar
top_signals.summary: The moves come as oil traders monitor the prospect of U.S.-Iran talks in Doha on Tuesday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Stocks Head for Best Quarter in Six Years | The Pulse 6/30/2026
top_signals.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Torsten Slok, Apollo Global Management Chief Economist; Jim O'Neil, UK House of Lords Member & Former Goldman Sachs Asset Management Chairman; Tobias Adrian, IMF Financial Counsellor & Director of the Monetary and Capital Markets Department; Beata Javorcik, European Bank for Reconstruction and Development Chief Economist    (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Strategy heads for eleventh losing month in twelve as bitcoin weakness continues
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Starmer stumps up half the amount demanded by defence chiefs
top_signals.summary: Sir Keir Starmer has coughed up just half of the amount of funding military officials requested in the long-awaited Defence Investment Plan.  During a speech led by defence secretary Dan Jarvis, Chancellor Rachel Reeves and the outgoing Prime Minister, ministers said they made the “right choices” and defended plans to back investment on drones and [...]
